content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes an integer as input and determines whether it is an even or odd number. Print "Even" if the number is even and "Odd" if it is odd.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'Even' if N is even, otherwise print 'Odd'. Each answer should be on a new line.

solution.approach: The key insight is to use the modulus operator (%) to find the remainder when N is divided by 2. If the remainder is 0, then N is even; otherwise, it is odd. This approach works because all even numbers can be represented as 2k, where k is an integer, and all odd numbers can be represented as 2k + 1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is a constant amount of space to store the input and output.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int N; if(scanf("%d",&N)!=1) break; if(N%2==0) printf("Even\n"); else printf("Odd\n"); } return 0; }
code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t=int(data[0])
    out=[]
    idx=1
    for _ in range(t):
        n=int(data[idx]); idx+=1
        out.append('Even' if n%2==0 else 'Odd')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
code.JavaScript: const lines = require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf-8').trim().split('\n'); let i=0; const T = parseInt(lines[i++]); let res = []; for(let j=0;j<T;j++){ const N = parseInt(lines[i++]); res.push(N%2===0?'Even':'Odd'); } console.log(res.join('\n'));
